singapore.title: Pain is part of her everyday life. But at 25, she’s fighting to do more than just ‘trying to survive’
singapore.summary: Nay Chi’s multiple chronic conditions have affected her studies, work, relationships at home and the kinds of daily decisions many other Singaporeans may take for granted. Her story is told in On The Red Dot.

singapore.summary: Last year, about 35 per cent of corruption-related reports received by the Corrupt Practices Investigation Bureau were anonymous, while nearly a quarter of investigations stemmed from anonymous tip-offs.

us.title: Massachusetts voters to decide marijuana repeal question after challenge rejected
us.summary: Massachusetts voters will decide in November if legal marijuana will be repealed after a challenge to the proposal was rejected Tuesday. The State Ballot Law Commission overruled an objection to the petition seeking to end the Bay State’s recreational cannabis law, allowing the proposal to be printed as a question on the ballot. The commission’s…

us.title: Lindell, Tafoya widening leads in Minnesota GOP primaries: Poll

us.title: 6 Democrats cross aisle, join GOP to back annual defense policy bill

The Democrats who backed the 2027 National Defense Authorization Act (NDAA) all have a reputation of breaking with their party on key issues and many are running for reelection in…

The plaintiff identified only as “R.K.C.” had brought claims against YouTube, Meta, Snap…

us.title: US, Saudis sign nuclear partnership
us.summary: The Trump administration on Wednesday announced what it described as a peaceful nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, hailing it as an economic triumph. But critics and nuclear experts have raised concerns around safeguards to prevent Riyadh from developing a nuclear weapon. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and his Saudi counterpart, the Minister of Energy His Royal Highness (HRH) Prince…

us.title: In South Dakota, public media endures a year after federal funding was wiped out

asia.title: US Congress explores new tariffs, sanctions to counter China’s shipbuilding dominance
asia.summary: US lawmakers are examining tariffs, port fees and sanctions targeting China’s shipbuilding industry, as US Congress searches for ways to rebuild America’s severely diminished commercial fleet without increasing costs for US importers and consumers. At a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing on Wednesday, members of both parties described China’s dominance of commercial shipbuilding as an economic and national security vulnerability, but witnesses cautioned that penalties alone would do little.

asia.title: US unveils civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
asia.summary: The US on Wednesday announced a landmark deal with Saudi Arabia that would establish a civilian nuclear programme in the Gulf country, with Washington insisting the agreement includes safeguards against non-proliferation. The move comes as the US under President Donald Trump fights a war with Iran that began in large part over concerns with Tehran’s nuclear programme – an issue that remains a key point of contention during now-stalled peace talks. Two agreements – one on “peaceful nuclear...

asia.title: What is Beijing thinking? Why Washington is struggling to understand its rival
asia.summary: After Donald Trump’s high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping in May, the idea of US-China “strategic stability” appeared in readouts from both sides. While the White House framed this vision as a “constructive relationship of strategic stability on the basis of fairness and reciprocity”, Beijing said only that it was a “constructive bilateral relationship of strategic stability”. China watchers zoomed in on the phrase – especially the Chinese version – debating what it could reveal or conceal about..

asia.title: Chile hunts for copper buyers beyond China with US$100 billion mining plan
asia.summary: Chile wants to widen the group of countries buying its copper and attract about US$100 billion in mining investment over the next decade, its foreign minister said on Wednesday, in the most explicit move yet by the new government in Santiago to reduce its exposure to Chinese demand. “The market is highly concentrated and we need to broaden the number of places that buy our copper,” Francisco Perez Mackenna said at a Bloomberg summit in Singapore, part of a tour through the Asia-Pacific. He said.

asia.title: US not getting enough critical minerals from China, Greer says as Xi-Trump meeting looms
asia.summary: Washington is still not receiving sufficient critical mineral exports from China, US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said on Wednesday, even as top American and Chinese officials met to finalise key economic deliverables for President Xi Jinping’s expected visit to the White House. “We are getting a flow of critical minerals from China. It’s not as much as we would want. It’s not at the pace we would want, but we are getting them,” Greer said during his testimony before the Senate Finance...

asia.title: How China and Indonesia can build a truly sturdy partnership
asia.summary: In April 1955, amid the Cold War, 304 representatives across 29 Asian and African nations convened in Bandung, Indonesia, to discuss the principles of a more complex future world order. Among them were India, China and host Indonesia. While India would go on to champion the non-aligned movement at the 1961 Belgrade Conference, Mao Zedong’s China and Sukarno’s Indonesia were more sceptical of the colonial, Western order. Then the more US-sympathetic Suharto seized power in Indonesia in 1966....

briefing: Global markets are watching the US Congress as it explores new tariffs and sanctions to counter China's dominance in shipbuilding, with lawmakers seeking to rebuild America's commercial fleet without increasing costs for importers and consumers. In Singapore, Prime Minister Wong is forming a new Cabinet line-up that bears his stamp, with first-term MPs getting fast-tracked through the political ranks. Meanwhile, the US has unveiled a landmark civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, which includes safeguards against non-proliferation, and the Dow Jones Index is likely to be influenced by these geopolitical developments. The technology sector is also abuzz with news that Meta won't have to face the next planned social media addiction trial, after the plaintiff dropped the case just days before the trial was set to begin.

digests.singapore: The recent Cabinet reshuffle in Singapore reflects Prime Minister Wong's efforts to build a strong and trustworthy team, with a focus on promoting first-term MPs and consolidating the 4G leadership while paving the way for emerging 5G leaders. Meanwhile, other local issues include the importance of anonymous tip-offs in the fight against corruption, with about 35% of corruption-related reports being anonymous, and the need for preventive measures such as designing buildings with maintenance features to cut upkeep and manpower costs. Singapore has also retained its position as having the world's most powerful passport, allowing visa-free or visa-on-arrival entry to 192 destinations.
digests.us: Massachusetts voters will decide on a marijuana repeal question in November, while measles cases have reached a 35-year high in the US. In politics, several Democratic lawmakers crossed party lines to support a $1.15 trillion annual defense policy bill, and a new poll shows Mike Lindell and Michele Tafoya gaining momentum in Minnesota GOP primaries. Additionally, the US has signed a nuclear partnership with Saudi Arabia, sparking concerns about safeguards to prevent nuclear weapon development.
digests.asia: The US is taking steps to counter China's dominance in various sectors, including shipbuilding, with Congress exploring tariffs and sanctions, while also pursuing deals with other countries, such as a civilian nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia. Meanwhile, China's intentions and actions continue to be a subject of scrutiny, with the US struggling to understand its rival's thinking, particularly with regards to the concept of "strategic stability". Other countries, like Chile, are seeking to reduce their dependence on China, with Chile aiming to diversify its copper exports and attract new investments, amidst ongoing tensions in West Asia, where the US has warned of retaliatory actions against Iran.
digests.others: Tensions in the Middle East are escalating, with Yemen's Houthis claiming attacks on Saudi oil tankers and the US threatening to target Iranian infrastructure in response to alleged attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The US is also strengthening its ties with Saudi Arabia through a landmark nuclear deal, while Iran has demonstrated its military capabilities with precise missile strikes. Meanwhile, international diplomacy is ongoing, with US Senator Rubio meeting his Chinese counterpart to discuss managing tensions and the UN preparing to choose a new Secretary-General amidst global challenges.
digests.tech: The technology and innovation landscape is marked by developments in social media, software, and automotive industries, with Meta avoiding a trial over social media addiction and Tesla experiencing a rebound in revenues. Meanwhile, advancements in fields like text-to-SQL and malleable computing are being explored, with discussions around the challenges of real-world data stores and the potential of Emacs. Additionally, releases such as Safari Technology Preview 248 and the introduction of original Xbox backward compatibility to Windows PCs demonstrate ongoing efforts to improve and expand existing technologies.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the upheaval of traditional power structures, with capitalism's status hierarchy being rewritten and new billionaires emerging in China, while also noting the threat of cheap Chinese AI labs to American dominance. Meanwhile, the publication critiques nostalgic leadership, such as Britain's new prime minister, and warns of the dangers of disregarding voters' voices, as seen in Nicaragua. The articles also touch on the shifting economic landscape, including the impact of inflation and the soaring demand for open-weight AI models.
